cross_references.rule: stance must align with quality_score: buy (score>=70), hold (score 50-69), avoid (score<50)
if stance=buy, prior_outputs.04_moat.durability_rating_0_to_5 must be >= 3 (decent moat required)

grounding_checks.description: Buy stance requires quality_score >= 65 (high conviction threshold) and prior moat durability >= 3
Avoid stance should align with either low quality score (<50) or weak moat (<=1) or high risk score (>=4)
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: stance='avoid' AND (quality_score >= 50 AND durability_rating >= 2 AND risk_rating < 4)

grounding_checks.description: quality_score_0_to_100 should decompose into six components (moat_durability 0-25, revenue_quality 0-15, pricing_power 0-15, industry_structure 0-15, management_allocation 0-20, balance_sheet 0-10) that sum to output score
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: quality_score component breakdown is missing or does not aggregate correctly

grounding_checks.description: Moat component (0-25) should not exceed prior_outputs.04_moat.durability_rating_0_to_5 * 5; quality_score should not exceed prior_outputs.04_moat.durability_rating_0_to_5 * 10 + 45
Management allocation component should be anchored to prior_outputs.05_management.rating_0_to_5 * 4 (0-20 scale)
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: management component appears disconnected from prior sprint rating

grounding_checks.description: falsifiers field must contain at least one specific, testable falsifier (length >= 50 chars, not generic)
key_points array should reference evidence from prior sprints (business model, unit economics, industry, moat, management, risks, peers)
grounding_checks.contradiction_rule: key_points are original claims not grounded in prior sprint evidence

synthesis_quality.prompt: Does the thesis coherently synthesize all 7 prior sprints into a unified investment recommendation? Does it explain WHY the company is buy/hold/avoid, tying moat durability, management quality, balance sheet resilience, industry structure, and valuation together? Or does it feel like disconnected fragments?
synthesis_quality.score_5: Seamless synthesis; each component clearly contributes to final stance; reader understands the core thesis
synthesis_quality.score_3: Mostly synthesized; 1-2 components feel disconnected or underweighted
synthesis_quality.score_1: Disjointed; reads like independent conclusions with weak connecting logic

quality_score_calibration.prompt: Is the 0-100 quality_score realistically calibrated and explicitly decomposed into the six weighted components (moat 25%, revenue quality 15%, pricing power 15%, industry structure 15%, management 20%, balance sheet 10%)? Does the arithmetic make sense and reflect the actual strength demonstrated across prior sprints?
quality_score_calibration.score_5: Decomposition fully transparent; arithmetic correct; calibration realistic per evidence
quality_score_calibration.score_3: Mostly correct; 1-2 component allocations seem off or decomposition is vague
quality_score_calibration.score_1: Missing decomposition, arithmetic errors, or score wildly misaligned with evidence

falsifiers_quality.prompt: Are the falsifiers (thesis-breaking events) specific, testable, and material? Or are they vague and generic? Could an investor actually monitor whether these events occur?
falsifiers_quality.score_5: 3+ specific falsifiers with clear trigger conditions; investor can measure them
falsifiers_quality.score_3: 1-2 falsifiers present; mostly specific but 1 is somewhat vague

consistency_with_prior_sprints.prompt: Does the thesis contradict any findings from the 7 prior sprints? Are references to moat durability, management rating, industry verdict, risk score, or peer positioning accurate?
consistency_with_prior_sprints.score_5: Zero contradictions; all references to prior sprints are accurate
consistency_with_prior_sprints.score_3: Minor inaccuracy (1 misquote or slight mischaracterization)
consistency_with_prior_sprints.score_1: Clear contradictions or material misrepresentations of prior findings

framework_focus: Apply Rule #1 Four Ms + QVG lens to synthesize all 7 prior sprints. Quality_score_0_to_100 must explicitly decompose into six anchored components: Moat & Durability (0-25, = durability_rating * 5), Revenue Quality (0-15, from business_profile recurrence + unit_economics), Pricing Power (0-15, anchored to pricing_power_score * 15), Industry Structure (0-15, from industry verdict: good=12-15, neutral=7-11, poor=0-6), Management & Allocation (0-20, = management_rating * 4), Balance Sheet Resilience (0-10, = balance_sheet_score * 2). Stance thresholds: buy (score>=70 AND moat>=3 AND management>=3), hold (score 50-69), avoid (score<50 OR moat<=1 OR risk>=4). Time horizon reflects business durability (shorter for commodities, longer for network-effect platforms). Falsifiers are thesis-breaking events with clear trigger conditions. The 14/20 LLM threshold is the highest of any sprint and reflects synthesis cognitive load — incoherent thesis failures are not recoverable downstream.
notes: 08_thesis is the synthesis sprint — it depends on all 7 prior sprints and produces the final investment recommendation. It uses Claude Sonnet (not Haiku) due to the synthesis cognitive load. The quality_score_0_to_100 must decompose explicitly into six weighted components: Moat & Durability (0-25, anchored to 04_moat.durability_rating * 5), Revenue Quality (0-15, from business_profile recurrence + unit_economics), Pricing Power (0-15, anchored to quality_scores.pricing_power_score * 15), Industry Structure (0-15, anchored to 03_industry.verdict: good=12-15, neutral=7-11, poor=0-6), Management & Allocation (0-20, anchored to 05_management.rating_0_to_5 * 4), Balance Sheet Resilience (0-10, anchored to four_ms.balance_sheet * 2). Stance thresholds: buy (score>=70 AND moat>=3 AND management>=3), hold (score 50-69), avoid (score<50 OR moat<=1). The 14/20 LLM threshold is the highest of any sprint and reflects the synthesis nature of this output — incoherent thesis failures are not recoverable downstream.
